title: Willis Sustainable Fuels selects FT CANS to boost Sustainable Aviation Fuel production in the UK
content: Johnson Matthey (JM), a global leader in sustainable technologies, today announced that Willis Sustainable Fuels (WSF) has selected JM and bp's award-winning Fischer Tropsch (FT) CANSâ„¢ technology for WSF's sustainable aviation fuel (SAF) project in Teesside, Northeast England. The project, which is expected to be operational in 2028, plans to be the first of its kind in the UK. The facility intends to use a biomethane feedstock, processed into syngas using JM's proven reforming technology. This syngas will feed the JM/bp FT CANS technology to produce synthetic crude oil that can be upgraded and blended to SAF.
author: Analysts Account
